item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So great scp, but it's mentioned the instances of SCP 3508 don't turn anomalous until removed from their packaging but seem to already be animate in other parts? "unpacking themselves in retail stores"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This lowkey reminds me of that one Powerpuff Girls villain where they used their cuteness to get away from robbing banks and that kinda shit. That, but more terrifying in a Fridge Horror way.

That voice acting makes me wanna punch the plushies, though. Time to fight against the stuffed animal apocalypse!

Take your upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not clicking that audio file again. +1. Excellent audio editing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Darn typos. Thanks for pointing it out!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Of course, there's always one that slips by! Thank you dearly!

As for the audio…

OwO

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
To date, only 5 Star-Level events have occurred to date

Saying “to date” twice is a bit odd sounding to me, but other than that I’ll +1 the psychotic stuffed animals.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A man wakes up tied to a chair, surrounded by stuffed animals brandishing various torture implements.

"It's just one of those days…"

Laugh track, end credits.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
We've all been there: A cuddly toy has tortured you to death for being an adult.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Murdertoys were himself, I believe. The other sound effects were stock taken from various sources, and the torture came from various games. (Correct me if I’m wrong.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, ATF seems really furry fandom based, but there isn't that much info to discern yet.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Instances of SCP-3508 will stage riots between the hours of 18:00 and 02:00 in both commercial and residential areas inhabited by individuals associated with typical Heart-Level event targets.

Heart-Level Events aren't mentioned anywhere other than here; did you mean Protest-level?

Also where the FUCK did you get that audio? I… I am disturbed. Very, very disturbed. Take your upvote while I take a cold shower.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a very well done article (which was expected, considering the quality of "OwO What's this" and "Mobile Task Furries"). The learning of the stuffed animals feels natural and the way they react to being separated from their "best friends" escalates at the right pace.

And then the audio.

I was absolutely horrified. As soon as the circular saw started, my eyes peeled right open. I might STILL be in shock and it's been about 10 minutes.

+1. Please keep your squishy plushies away from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sweet.

I could be completely wrong about this but all their stuff seems to be kid/marketing anomalies gone wrong. What’s the jist with them?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Definitely a good point, but in my opinion, edgy shit can be done tactfully if you are a good writer.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is exactly what I didn't like about this. Different strokes for different folks, but reminding me of something edgy schoolkids like is something I generally consider a bad thing.

The audio logs were good quality enough to not downvote though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: ATF next GOI confirmed

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Isn’t this the same group that made that creepy furry nightmare-fuel SCP?

item.title: NO
item.content:encoded:
Never make me hear that audio again. I prefer not being scared to death by the voices I read them in.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not even close to being the first.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Damn, this is the first recently created SCP that's gotten above 10 upvotes in one day. Good job I liked it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is an extremely well done, and well made SCP. It's well written, and the amount of content just makes me smile. I love nice and meaty articles like this, you've got so much great content. And this SCP goes from something seemingly innocent, to really creepy and terrifying in a nice smooth manner. I love how this SCP can operate as a large group, and I love that they've got some form of leader with SCP-3508-Prime.

Also that voice acting! It's not something I get to see often, but it really adds to this SCP. I enjoyed the voice acting, as it almost makes this SCP feel more real and believable. +1. I wish I could give you +2 though, for the sheer amount of effort and quality this SCP provides.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It totally is! 'Cupcakes', thank you!
I couldn't for the life of me remember it's name. I'd forgotten it was fanfiction too. It's so weirdly memorable, the torture scene in this SCP totally captures the feel of it as well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I believe the MLP torture "animation" you are talking about is that fan fiction Cupcakes, correct?

Like, holy shit, the stuff edgy children like me liked back then. I've since grown up from the brony fandom but damn if those memories of watching edgy pinkie pie cut up rainbow dash like a tuna fish sandwich aren't still stuck in my mind.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I absolutely love this! Articles like this make me wish I could +2. It gives me Happy (the graphic novel and later TV show) vibes, as well as that one MLP torture animation ("Cupcakes", thank you pastarasta1!) edgy kids used to show each other in school. I never thought adorable torture sounds would make me nostalgic.

Anyway, I think the second letter might be my second favourite part of this. The audio was my favourite, obviously. "It smiles up at him with childlike innocence" is such an absurd line (and almost certainly a break in clinical tone) but I love it anyway.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh my god.

+1.

item.title: it done
item.content:encoded:
I've never edited audio before in my life. Not once.

Props to everyone in the SCP Chat for helping with this skip! I couldn't have done it without you guys.
